meaning: Public connection matrix for Nipmod platform paths. Live means Nipmod controls and has verified the path. MCP ready means a host can connect to the local Nipmod MCP server. Under review and Candidate require outside owner action before stronger claims are valid.

statusLegend.meaning: Nipmod controls the path and the smoke or sync proof is passing.
The host can use the local Nipmod MCP server and runtime checks pass.
Nipmod prepared the integration, but a third party must review or accept it before native support can be claimed.
The platform or project fits the package archive model, but owner review has not happened yet.

connections.name: Gitlawb
connections.scope: First canonical source anchor for signed source history, owner claim verification and package publishing.
connections.proofLevel: Pushed, synced and checked against the current released commit. Owner Package Claims are verified separately and missing proofs are reported without being counted as verified.
connections.externalDependency: No external approval is required for the current Nipmod owned Gitlawb path.

connections.name: GitHub
connections.scope: Public mirror, CI surface, docs, connection kits and developer review path.
connections.proofLevel: Main branch matches the released commit and the repo is public.
connections.externalDependency: No external approval is required for the current Nipmod owned GitHub repo.

connections.name: MCP
connections.scope: Local stdio server exposing search, view, inspect, install plan, controlled install, audit, SBOM and proof tools.
connections.proofLevel: The MCP tool list is checked by platform and system readiness tests.
connections.externalDependency: No marketplace approval is required to run the local stdio MCP server.

connections.name: Codex
connections.scope: Codex can register Nipmod as a local stdio MCP server through the Nipmod setup command.
connections.proofLevel: Host smoke passes through codex mcp get in the platform readiness check.
connections.externalDependency: No native marketplace claim is made. The current path is local MCP setup.

connections.name: Claude Code
connections.scope: Claude Code can load the project .mcp.json and connect to the local Nipmod MCP server.
connections.proofLevel: Host smoke passes through claude mcp list using the committed .mcp.json.
connections.externalDependency: No native marketplace claim is made. The current path is local MCP setup.

connections.name: OpenCode
connections.scope: OpenCode can load opencode.json and connect to the local Nipmod MCP server.
connections.proofLevel: Host smoke passes through opencode mcp list using the committed opencode.json.
connections.externalDependency: No native marketplace claim is made. The current path is local MCP setup.

connections.name: Hermes
connections.scope: Hermes Agent can load MCP servers through config.yaml. Nipmod now has a prepared local MCP setup path for that runtime.
connections.proofLevel: Nipmod setup hermes writes the mcp_servers.nipmod config and the local Nipmod MCP server is checked independently. A real Hermes runtime smoke is still pending.
connections.smokeCommand: hermes chat, then /reload-mcp and ask Hermes to list Nipmod MCP tools
connections.externalDependency: A real Hermes runtime smoke and upstream acknowledgement are required before stronger Hermes support wording is used.

connections.name: Bankr
connections.scope: Bankr compatible skill, free service map and agent proof workflow are prepared without wallet actions.
connections.proofLevel: Local proof commands pass. Native Bankr catalog acceptance and authenticated Agent API smoke are external.
connections.externalDependency: Bankr must review or merge the skill before native Bankr support can be claimed.

connections.name: Aeon
connections.scope: Aeon has a large public skill system that fits a future Nipmod external skill collection.
connections.proofLevel: Candidate only. No Aeon owner approval or published Nipmod package collection is claimed.
connections.smokeCommand: Review candidate repo and prepare owner approved skill collection metadata
connections.externalDependency: Aeon owner review is required before publishing Aeon skills through Nipmod.

notClaimed: Native marketplace approval for every platform
Every Gitlawb package has a verified Owner Package Claim proof
Bankr accepted the skill into the Bankr skills repo
Aeon approved or published a Nipmod skill collection
Hermes runtime smoke has passed without a local Hermes install and auth
MCP ready means native platform partnership
